name: Mim Inter American Bus Trad
recent_news: No public news or web presence identified. Listing only appears via D&B/business directory data; PO Box-only address with no website suggests very small or potentially inactive operator. A loosely related 'Inter American Transportation' Florida entity was dissolved 2017 per Sunbiz, but link to this name is unconfirmed.
fit_notes: Very small (3 employees, ~$134K revenue), PO Box address, no confirmed school district contracts or website. Too small/uncertain to qualify as a Summit tuck-in target absent confirmation it is an active school-bus operator.

name: Global School Bus Inc
recent_news: No press coverage, website, or NYSDOT bus-operator profile entry found for the Kirkville, NY 13082 address. Name is generic and not to be confused with NYC-area 'Global Transportation' (globaltransp.com) which operates a separate business.
fit_notes: Residential-style Syracuse-suburb address (Kirkville), no phone listed, no website, only 3 employees. Likely a single-bus owner-operator; not an attractive Summit tuck-in. Verify activity via NYSDOT bus operator registry before any outreach.

name: Fisher Brothers Inc
recent_news: July 2023: Fisher Brothers and Krise Transportation initially walked away from MCSD when contracts were not renewed, creating a Mifflin County transportation crisis. March 2025: MCSD board recommended new 5-year pupil-transportation agreement with Fisher Brothers (7/1/2025-6/30/2030); agreement was rescinded and re-approved in revised form June 2025, restoring Fisher Brothers as a core MCSD contractor through 2030.
fit_notes: Genuine family-owned PA school-bus operator since 1959 with ~27 buses serving Mifflin County SD on a fresh 5-year contract through 2030 - exactly the profile Summit targets. Recent contract drama suggests succession/strategic-options conversations may be welcome. Strong tuck-in candidate in central PA; reach out to Max Fisher.

name: Gateway Transportation Inc
recent_news: No M&A activity identified. Active operator with public Birdeye reviews; some customer complaints relating to driver behavior/safety. Long-tenured President Morris D. Strauss has led the company since founding in 1988.
fit_notes: Independent family-owned Capital Region NY school-bus contractor (est. 1988, ~30 employees, ~$1.6M revenue per Manta) with founder-president still at helm after ~38 years - classic succession profile. Reasonable Summit tuck-in candidate in upstate NY; small-to-mid scale but in a fragmented Schenectady/Albany cluster useful for density.

name: Cameron Bus Svc
recent_news: No web presence, news, or NYC Office of Pupil Transportation (OPT) contractor listing identified. Ozone Park residential address and 3-employee count suggest a single-bus owner-operator rather than a contracted NYC OPT vendor.
fit_notes: Cannot confirm as an active school-bus contractor; no phone, no website, residential Queens address. Below Summit's typical tuck-in threshold and unverifiable without primary outreach. Deprioritize.
